item.title: Financial Stability Board chair warns of stability "triple whammy" as funding conditions tighten
item.description: The global financial system has so far absorbed the economic shock caused by the ​Iran war, partly thanks to post-2008 crisis ‌reforms, but rising funding costs may exacerbate growing stress in other parts of the system, the chair of ​the Financial Stability Board warned the G20 ​on Monday.

item.title: World Liberty investor Justin Sun claims Trump crypto venture 'secretly' installed tool to freeze user holdings
item.description: A major investor in U.S. President Donald Trump's World Liberty Financial crypto venture has claimed ​that the firm "secretly" implemented a tool to unilaterally freeze and restrict private holdings of its WLFI token.

item.title: NY Fed says paper loss on bond holdings shrank last year
item.description: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York said on Monday that ​the U.S. central bank had a smaller unrealized loss on its expansive bond holdings last year relative to 2024.

item.title: Anthropic talking to the Trump administration about its next AI model, co-founder says
item.description: Anthropic is discussing its frontier AI model Mythos with the Trump administration, the firm's co-founder said on Monday, even ​after the Pentagon cut off business with the U.S. ‌AI company following a contract dispute.

item.title: Brazil's top labor inspector fired for adding China's BYD to 'dirty list,' sources say
item.description: Brazil's secretary ​of labor inspection was fired for adding Chinese automaker BYD to a registry of employers accused of ‌subjecting workers to conditions described by the government as similar to slavery, two people close to the matter told Reuters.

item.title: AI-boosted hacks with Anthropic’s Mythos could have dire consequences for banks
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos, a new AI model the company and cybersecurity experts warn could supercharge complex cyberattacks, ​poses significant challenges to the banking industry with its legacy technology systems, experts said in the days following the model's announcement.

item.title: Middle East war drives up Codelco copper costs in Chile
item.description: Price surges due to the ​Middle East war have raised costs for ‌the world's top-producing copper miner Codelco, yet the Chilean state-run firm is still on track to meet its ​2026 production target, Chairman Maximo Pacheco told Reuters ​on Monday.

item.title: Texas attorney general probes Lululemon over potential 'forever chemicals' in its activewear
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has launched an investigation into athleisure brand Lululemon over the potential presence of "forever chemicals" ​in its activewear, he said on Monday in a ‌post on social-media platform X.

item.title: Exclusive: Manulife Investment Management eyes Cellnex Swiss business, sources say
item.description: Spain's Cellnex is in talks to sell its stake in its Swiss ​unit to Toronto-listed Manulife Financial Corporation's investment management ‌unit, according to two sources with knowledge of the situation.

item.title: Hollywood stars sign open letter opposing Paramount-Warner Bros deal
item.description: Over 1,000 filmmakers, actors and industry professionals signed an open letter opposing Warner Bros Discovery's ​proposed $110 billion merger with Paramount Skydance, warning it ‌would reduce competition and deepen consolidation in the U.S. media sector.

item.title: US IPO hopefuls forge ahead with listing plans amid market swings
item.description: A slew of companies across sectors, from biotech to real estate investment trust, launched IPO roadshows on ​Monday, as issuers prepare to go ahead with their listing ‌plans amid the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Meta poised to surpass Google in digital ad revenue for first time, report says
item.description: Meta Platforms is projected to surpass Alphabet's Google in digital advertising revenue globally by the end of 2026, and ​dethrone the search-engine behemoth in the lucrative business, according to ‌Emarketer.

item.title: LVMH flags sales impact of Iran war in blow to hopes for luxury revival
item.description: French luxury giant LVMH suffered a heavy impact from the Middle Eastern conflict, it said on Monday, as sales ​fell in the Gulf and many rich tourists from the region also stopped spending in Europe.

item.title: J.P.Morgan, Morgan Stanley urge buying the dip as US earnings stay resilient
item.description: Wall Street brokerages J.P.Morgan and Morgan Stanley said recent market weakness has created opportunities for long-term investors, arguing that resilient corporate earnings growth could cushion the fallout from the Middle East ​conflict.

item.title: Bezos-backed EV firm Slate Auto raises $650 million
item.description: Electric vehicle company Slate Auto on Monday said it has raised $650 million in ​a Series C funding round, as ‌the Jeff Bezos-backed firm gears up to deliver its first vehicles late this year.

item.title: Uber faces second driver sexual assault trial following $8.5 million verdict
item.description: After a key setback in the first trial of its kind before a federal jury, Uber ​is expected to return to court on Tuesday to face a second woman's claims that she was sexually assaulted by ‌a driver she booked through the app.

item.title: China's Geely launches new hybrid system to challenge Japanese dominance
item.description: Geely Auto on Monday launched its i-HEV Intelligent Hybrid system, aiming to challenge the dominance of Japanese automakers in the conventional ​hybrid vehicle segment by improving fuel efficiency and intelligence capabilities.

item.title: Iran war weighs on global economy as IMF meeting starts
item.description: Alarm over the impact of the Iran war on the global economy grew on Monday with more countries announcing emergency support measures to combat rising energy costs, while others appealed for international ​support.

item.title: US existing home sales drop to nine-month low in March amid tight supply
item.description: U.S. existing home sales fell to a nine-month low in March amid tight inventory ​and growing concerns over the labor market, and a recent increase in mortgage rates because of the war with ‌Iran could limit activity this year.

item.title: Dutch regulator notifies European Commission of plan to seek EU approval for Tesla's FSD
item.description: The Dutch vehicle ​authority RDW ‌has notified the European ​Commission ​of its plan to ⁠seek ​European Union-wide ​approval for Tesla's Full-Self Driving ​system, Bernd ​van Nieuwenhoven, general ‌manager ⁠of type approvals at the ​regulator, ​told ⁠Reuters on Monday.

item.title: UK to probe Paramount-Warner Bros deal in coming weeks, competition watchdog says
item.description: Britain's antitrust watchdog is expected to launch an investigation into Paramount Skydance's planned $110 billion acquisition of ​Warner Bros Discovery in "the coming weeks," ‌a spokesperson said on Monday.

item.title: Roblox to introduce age-based accounts in child safety push
item.description: Videogame platform Roblox on Monday said it will create tailored accounts for young users in a push to shore ​up online safety, as it deals with intense global ‌scrutiny over concerns of child endangerment.

item.title: Tesco partners with Adobe to ramp up AI‑driven personalised marketing
item.description: Tesco, Britain's biggest food retailer, has partnered with U.S. software group Adobe to deepen its use of artificial intelligence ​in analysing customer data, aiming to boost sales through ‌more personalised marketing, the groups said on Monday.

item.title: OPEC lowers second-quarter global oil demand forecast on Iran war
item.description: OPEC on Monday lowered its forecast for world oil demand in the second quarter by 500,000 barrels per day, the producer group's monthly oil report showed in its first public assessment of the Iran war's impact ​on the market.

item.title: Bill Ackman kicks off roadshow for combined IPO of Pershing Square, new fund
item.description: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square kicked off a roadshow for the U.S. initial ​public offerings of the billionaire investor's management company and a new ‌fund on Monday.

item.title: Conagra Brands names J.M. Smucker executive John Brase as new CEO
item.description: Conagra Brands named J.M. Smucker ​executive John Brase as ‌its new CEO, succeeding Sean Connolly, who will step down after ​more than a decade ​at the helm of the ⁠packaged food maker.

item.title: Goldman Sachs posts higher profit on strength in dealmaking, equities trading
item.description: Goldman Sachs posted a rise in first-quarter profit on Monday, as the Wall Street giant benefited from strength in dealmaking and equities trading.

item.title: Social media platforms need to stop never-ending scrolling, UK's Starmer says
item.description: Social media platforms such as Instagram and TikTok need to take action to stop young users wasting hours ​mindlessly scrolling never-ending videos, British Prime Minister Keir ‌Starmer said on Monday.

item.title: REIT National Healthcare aims $1.1 billion in valuation in US IPO
item.description: National Healthcare Properties said on Monday it was targeting a valuation of about $1.1 billion in ​its U.S. initial public offering, the second real ‌estate investment trust to hit the new listings markets in recent weeks.

item.title: Morning Bid: Blockade takes its toll
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today
